channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Why Trump and others are worried about the bond market
item.description: President Trump’s tariff plans have raised jitters about the stability of the bond market as demand quickly dropped before he implemented his 90-day pause on most tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: Prince Harry meets war victims in unannounced visit to Ukraine
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry met with war victims on Thursday during an unannounced visit to Ukraine as part of his ongoing work with wounded veterans, a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Democrat says Trump-Xi in 'testosterone battle' with tariff war
item.description: Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.) on Thursday likened the U.S. tariff war with China to a “testosterone battle” between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Yellen: Trump 'has taken a wrecking ball' to economy with trade war
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen, who served under former President Biden, said President Trump “has taken a wrecking ball” to the economy.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Khalil lawyers blast Rubio court filing that claims power to deport noncitizens over speech
item.description: Mahmoud Khalil’s attorneys on Thursday blasted a court filing from Secretary of State Marco Rubio claiming he has the ability to remove noncitizens for their speech and beliefs, a filing that came after an immigration judge demanded the government present evidence against the former Columbia Univers
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: A prisoner swap between Moscow and Washington frees Ksenia Karelina, a US-Russia dual national
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Moscow freed a Russian American convicted of treason in exchange for a Russian German man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S., a prisoner swap that was completed Thursday as the two countries met to repair ties.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US ambassador to Ukraine stepping down
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine Bridget Brink is stepping down from the role she held for nearly three years, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: African nations that faced steep Trump tariffs get some relief but mostly more uncertainty
item.description: African nations that were facing some of the steepest reciprocal tariffs from the Trump administration have been given a moment of relief with the pausing of the duties.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel releases 10 Palestinians detained from Gaza. They say they suffered abuse
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Ten Palestinians detained from Gaza by Israeli troops were freed and returned to the territory on Thursday, saying they had suffered constant abuse while imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Influencer Andrew Tate accused of pointing gun at woman's face ahead of London civil case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — One of four women suing influencer and self-styled misogynist Andrew Tate for sexual assault said he whipped her with a belt and once stuck a gun in her face, according to court documents that have been filed in London.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where things stand for Trump in global tariff battle
item.description: In the aftermath of Wednesday’s tariff whiplash, President Donald Trump is deciding exactly what he wants out of trade talks with as many as 75 nations in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taliban morality enforcers arrest men for having the wrong hairstyle or skipping mosque, UN says
item.description: The Taliban morality police in Afghanistan have detained men and their barbers over hairstyles and others for missing prayers at mosques during the holy month of Ramadan, a U.N. report said Thursday, six months after laws regulating people’s conduct came into effect.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Taiwan's president targets China influence, kicks out pro-Beijing agitators amid rising tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President William Lai promises an uncompressing stance against both domestic and foreign pro-CCP influence operations amid rising tensions between Taipei and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine 'coalition of the willing' presses ahead with force planning amid doubts about US support
item.description: Britain’s defense minister is pressing his counterparts from around 30 countries to forge ahead with plans to deploy troops to Ukraine to police any future peace agreement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 50 years later, Vietnam's battlefields draw retrospective veterans and other tourists
item.description: HO CHI MINH CITY, Vietnam (AP) — Hamburger Hill, Hue, the Ia Drang Valley, Khe Sanh: Some remember the Vietnam War battles from the headlines of the 1960s and 1970s, others from movies and history books.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and HAU DINH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian PM: Negotiations with Trump on economic, security relationship to begin after election
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday that negotiations with President Trump on the U.S.-Canada economic and security relationships will start after the Canadian federal election.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Famous North Korean assassin who became a pastor in the South following failed 1968 mission dies
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Kim Shin-jo, a prominent ex-North Korean commando who resettled in South Korea as a pastor after his daring mission to assassinate then South Korean President Park Chung-hee in 1968 failed, died on Wednesday. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The roof collapses at a Dominican Republic nightclub, killing at least 98 people
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the Dominican capital collapsed early Tuesday during a merengue concert attended by politicians, athletes and others, leaving at least 98 people dead and 160 injured, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills a journalist in a media tent outside a Gaza hospital
item.description: Israel struck tents outside two major hospitals in the Gaza Strip overnight, killing at least two people, including a local reporter, and wounding nine, including six journalists, Palestinian medics said. Separate strikes killed at least 15 others across the Gaza Strip, according to hospitals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US revokes visas for South Sudanese as civil war looms at home
item.description: Here’s a look at South Sudan, whose people had been granted temporary protected status by the U.S. because of insecurity at home. That status expires on May 3.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN global comms arm under fire for anti-Israel bias as critics call for reforms
item.description: The U.N.'s Department of Global Communications is under fire for a one-sided view of the Israel-Palestinian conflict, with critics saying it often sides with Palestinians at the expense of other conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China slaps a 34% tax on all US imports in retaliation for Trump's tariffs
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tax on all U.S. imports next week, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new tariffs that delivered the strongest response yet from Beijing to the American leader's trade war.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
